reasoning_model_1: The model fails critically on Content Accuracy. While it reads the target sentence '我终于解开了这道难题！原来关键在这里，只要用这个方法就能迎刃而解。', it fails to stop there. It proceeds to read the descriptive instructions/prompt text: '声音由高到低缓缓沉降，像钟声余韵渐消，尾音轻如叹息'. This is a severe hallucination/instruction-following failure where the model verbalizes the style description instead of applying it as a paralinguistic feature. Due to this inclusion of extra non-speech text, the audio is deemed completely incorrect regardless of the pitch performance.
reasoning_model_2: The model demonstrates perfect Content Accuracy, strictly reading only the required text: '我终于解开了这道难题！原来关键在这里，只要用这个方法就能迎刃而解。' without any hallucinations or extra words. Regarding the Paralinguistic Compliance (Pitch): The audio follows the instruction to start with a high pitch and gradually lower it. The phrase '我终于解开了这道难题' is delivered with a higher, excited pitch (00:00-00:02), and the pitch noticeably descends through the middle section, ending on a much lower, grounded pitch at '迎刃而解' (00:05-00:07). The transition is smooth and natural.
model_comparison: Model 1 (T1) suffers from a critical failure in content accuracy by reading out the prompt's style description ('声音由高到低...') as part of the speech, which automatically results in a failing score. Model 2 (T2) correctly filters the text, speaking only the target content. Furthermore, T2 successfully executes the dynamic pitch adjustment, starting high and ending low as requested. Therefore, Model 2 is the clear winner.
